name: quickserve
description: Zero-config local development server with Hot Module Replacement (HMR), automatic HTTPS, and QR code for mobile testing. Instantly preview HTML and MARKDOWN — no setup required.

quickserve.port.description: The local port number the server will listen on. If the port is in use, QuickServe may fail to start.

quickserve.publicAccess.description: Enable network access to allow other devices (phones, tablets, or other PCs) on your local network to connect to the server.

quickserve.https.markdownDescription:
### **HTTPS & SSL Configuration**
Enable HTTPS for testing features that demand a **Secure Context** (Service Workers, Clipboard API, etc.).

#### **Self-Healing Mode**
If `certPath` and `keyPath` are empty, QuickServe automatically manages security:
1. **With `mkcert`:** Generates a fully **trusted local certificate** (local install required).
2. **Fallback:** Generates a **standard self-signed certificate** (browser warning).

enable.description: Set to true to activate HTTPS.

certPath.description: Full path to custom SSL certificate file (.crt or .pem).

keyPath.description: Full path to custom SSL key file (.key).

quickserve.enableWatcher.markdownDescription: Start a background **file watcher** to monitor workspace changes. This is the core service required for both `Full Reload` and `HMR` to function.

quickserve.enableHMR.markdownDescription:
Enable **Hot Module Replacement (HMR)**. This allows CSS and assets to update instantly without a full page reload, preserving the current application state. 

> **Note:** This feature requires `quickserve.enableWatcher` to be enabled so the file watcher can detect changes.

quickserve.showStatusbar.description: Toggle the visibility of the QuickServe controls in the VS Code status bar.

quickserve.showInfoMessages.description: Show VS Code notification pop-ups for server lifecycle events (Start, Stop, Errors).

quickserve.showServerStatusOnStart.description: Automatically open and focus the QuickServe output channel to show the local URL and QR code when the server starts.

quickserve.openBrowser.description: Automatically open browser when the server starts.

scripts.compile: npm run check-types && npm run lint && node esbuild.js
scripts.package: npm run check-types && npm run lint && node esbuild.js --production
scripts.pretest: npm run compile-tests && npm run compile && npm run lint

author: Debanshu Panigrahi <debanshupanigrahi@gmail.com>
